sceneAudits.biggestGap: The original investigation floated above the kingdom's grain system and needed more depot texture, road infrastructure, and rider-contact.

place: King's Road split south of Millhaven
material: grain dust on sleeves and saddle maps
dialogue: route arguments over manifests and charcoal marks
road-analysis on horseback with maps snapping in the wind
exit: shadowed north road versus light still holding south

sceneAudits.biggestGap: The cleaned-depot reversal needed stronger physical evidence of recent erasure so the office and corpse read as engineered quiet, not generic emptiness.

place: rear office behind the false front
material: torn route strip with broken Barov wax
dialogue: corpse assessment tied to crystal work and floorboards
deduction beats tied to windows, shelves, and slate
exit: Barov entering as route pressure through surviving scraps

sceneAudits.scene: Scene 3 - Night Analysis and the South Road
sceneAudits.biggestGap: The closing analysis risked turning abstract; it needed waystation texture, map contact, and a world-bound memory of the trader to keep the chapter's tail alive.

place: stone waystation east of Millbrook Spur
southwest feeder road toward Strahnbrad and Andorhal
material: grain dust silvering wagon slats in memory
dialogue: route debate tied to palms on papers and shifting scraps
naming shift to Jaina grounded in working rhythm at the table
exit: the feeder road continuing south after the light goes out

risksFound: tail abstraction risk in the route-analysis scene
memory beat risked floating without enough trader texture
fixesApplied: kept the route debate pinned to the map, candle, watch rhythm, and horse noise
restored the old seller through wagon slats, coat, broken nose, and road-market knowledge
ended on the physical southbound feeder road rather than abstract resolve
